name: Glutathione
subtitle: L-Glutathione | Master Antioxidant Tripeptide
overview: Glutathione is the body's master antioxidant, a tripeptide produced naturally by the liver. It protects cells against free radicals, peroxides, and heavy metals while eliminating toxins including drugs and pollutants. Injectable glutathione bypasses gastrointestinal breakdown for superior bioavailability compared to oral supplements. Research supports benefits for neurological health, immune function, skin health, and anti-aging.
mechanism: Glutathione acts as the primary intracellular antioxidant, directly neutralizing reactive oxygen species and regenerating other antioxidants like vitamins C and E. It plays crucial roles in detoxification by conjugating with toxins for elimination, supports immune function through T-cell regulation, protects mitochondria, and maintains cellular redox balance. The thiol group of cysteine provides its antioxidant activity.
keyBenefits: Master antioxidant - protects against oxidative stress
Detoxification of heavy metals and toxins
Immune system support and T-cell function
Skin health and potential brightening effects

items.name: Oxidative Stress Reduction
items.description: Primary cellular antioxidant that neutralizes free radicals and protects against oxidative damage.

items.name: Heavy Metal Detoxification
items.description: Binds and facilitates elimination of heavy metals and environmental toxins.

items.name: Liver Support
items.description: Essential for hepatic detoxification pathways and liver protection.

items.name: Parkinson's Disease Support
items.description: IV glutathione showed improvements in mental function and speech quality in case studies.

items.name: Neuroprotection
items.description: Protects neurons against oxidative damage and supports brain health.

items.name: Immune Enhancement
items.description: Improved Th1 cytokines and immune markers in HIV patients with supplementation.

items.name: Skin Health
items.description: Supports skin health with potential brightening effects through melanin modulation.

items.name: Anti-Aging
items.description: Combats age-related oxidative stress and supports cellular longevity.

deliveryMethods.overview: Injectable glutathione is the most effective delivery method, bypassing GI breakdown for direct absorption. Subcutaneous injections are typically administered 2-3 times weekly. IV administration is fastest-acting but requires clinical supervision. Start with lower doses to assess tolerance.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly
Store in refrigerator, protect from light

deliveryMethods.overview: Oral glutathione has poor bioavailability due to GI breakdown. Liposomal formulations may offer improved absorption but are still less effective than injection.

interactions.notes: Both support cellular energy and antioxidant systems; commonly combined in longevity protocols.
Glutathione regenerates oxidized vitamin C, creating synergistic antioxidant effects.
Both protect mitochondria; different mechanisms complement each other.

timeline.effects: Improved energy and well-being may be noticed
Full benefits for skin, immunity, and cellular health

stopSigns: Signs of allergic reaction (rash, difficulty breathing)
monitoring: Consider zinc supplementation with long-term use

references.title: Randomized, double-blind, pilot evaluation of intravenous glutathione in Parkinson's disease
references.authors: Hauser RA, Lyons KE, McClain T, Carter S, Perlmutter D
references.keyFindings: 21 PD subjects randomized to IV glutathione 1,400 mg or placebo 3x weekly for 4 weeks. Well tolerated with no safety concerns. Preliminary data suggested possibility of mild symptomatic effect.

references.title: Liposomal Glutathione Supplementation Restores TH1 Cytokine Response to Mycobacterium tuberculosis Infection in HIV-Infected Individuals
references.authors: Ly J, Lagman M, Saing T, Singh MK, Tudella EV, Morris D, Anderson J, Daliva J, Ochoa C, Patel N, Venketaraman V
references.keyFindings: 13-week liposomal glutathione supplementation in HIV-infected individuals resulted in significant increases in plasma Th1 cytokines IL-12 and IFN-γ, restoring immune response.

references.title: Exploring the Safety and Efficacy of Glutathione Supplementation for Skin Lightening: A Narrative Review
references.keyFindings: Narrative review concluding glutathione has potential as a depigmenting agent. Oral supplementation shows significant but variable melanin reduction. IV use associated with safety concerns including anaphylaxis.

references.title: Glutathione metabolism and its implications for health
references.authors: Wu G, Fang YZ, Yang S, Lupton JR, Turner ND
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review establishing glutathione's roles in antioxidant defense, nutrient metabolism, regulation of gene expression, DNA/protein synthesis, cell proliferation and apoptosis, immune response, and protein glutathionylation.

latestResearch.title: Glutathione as a skin-lightening agent and in melasma: a systematic review
latestResearch.summary: Systematic review of 5 RCTs found oral glutathione at 250-500mg showed significant melanin index reduction vs placebo. Topical formulations also effective. IV use lacks standardized protocols.

latestResearch.title: Randomized Clinical Trial of How Long-Term Glutathione Supplementation Offers Protection from Oxidative Damage and Improves HbA1c in Elderly Type 2 Diabetic Patients
latestResearch.summary: Long-term oral glutathione supplementation in elderly type 2 diabetic patients significantly increased blood GSH levels, decreased 8-OHdG oxidative damage marker, and improved HbA1c within three months.

quickStats.typicalDose: 100-600 mg injectable (100-200mg wellness, 200-600mg intensive); IV dosing higher (600-1400mg clinical)
quickStats.frequency: 2-3 times weekly for subcutaneous; weekly for IV infusions
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: 2-8°C refrigerated, protect from light; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated, protect from light
